activities_projects.title: Analysing Constitutional Values
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the importance of constitutional values
steps: Read the Preamble of the Indian Constitution carefully
Identify the values mentioned such as justice, liberty, equality and fraternity
Write a paragraph explaining how these values help maintain democracy
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops analytical understanding of constitutional values

activities_projects.title: Case Study on Fundamental Rights
activities_projects.objective: Apply constitutional rights to real-life situations
steps: Imagine a situation where a person's Fundamental Rights are violated
Describe the situation clearly
Explain how the Constitution protects that person
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Applies constitutional provisions to practical situations

activities_projects.title: Debate on Equality
activities_projects.objective: Understand the significance of equality in democracy
steps: Form two groups in the class
Debate on the topic 'Equality is the foundation of democracy'
Write a summary of the main points discussed
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops critical thinking on democratic principles
activities_projects.evaluation: Quality of arguments and summary (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Comparing Different Constitutions
activities_projects.objective: Understand that different countries have different constitutions
steps: Find out about the Constitution of another country
List two similarities and two differences compared to the Indian Constitution
Explain what you learned from the comparison

activities_projects.title: Rights vs Responsibilities Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the relationship between rights and responsibilities
steps: List five Fundamental Rights mentioned in the Constitution
Write the responsibility associated with each right
Explain why rights cannot exist without responsibilities
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the balance between rights and duties

activities_projects.title: Constitution and Social Justice
activities_projects.objective: Examine how the Constitution promotes social justice
steps: Identify two examples in the textbook where the Constitution promotes justice or equality
Explain the situation described
Write how constitutional principles help solve the problem
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Analyses constitutional solutions to social problems

activities_projects.title: Mock Constitution Drafting
activities_projects.objective: Understand the process of making rules for a society
steps: Imagine you are forming a new school or club
Write five important rules that everyone must follow
Explain why each rule is necessary
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops understanding of rule-making in governance

activities_projects.title: Research on the Making of the Constitution
activities_projects.objective: Learn about the historical development of the Indian Constitution
steps: Find out when the Constitution of India was adopted
Write about the role of the Constituent Assembly
Explain why the drafting process was important
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the historical context of the Constitution

activities_projects.title: Critical Thinking: Constitution Without Rights
activities_projects.objective: Evaluate the importance of Fundamental Rights
steps: Imagine a country without Fundamental Rights
Write three problems that might arise in such a country
Explain how the Constitution prevents these problems
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops critical thinking about constitutional safeguards

activities_projects.title: Essay on the Importance of the Constitution
activities_projects.objective: Demonstrate comprehensive understanding of the Constitution
steps: Write a short essay explaining the importance of the Indian Constitution
Include ideas such as rights, equality, justice and democracy
Conclude with why citizens must respect the Constitution
